title: Dynaudio Unveils Updated Symphony Opus One Immersive Soundbar at CES 2025
bodyHtml:
Dynaudio Unveils Updated Symphony Opus One Immersive Soundbar at CES 2025

Dynaudio, a brand known for its dedication to premium sound solutions, is set to showcase the updated Symphony Opus One soundbar concept at CES 2025.

Following its initial reveal at CEDIA Expo 2024, the updated design marks a significant leap forward in all-in-one home audio systems. Attendees can experience the demo at Central Hall booth 21832.

‍

‍

A Vision for All-in-One Home Audio

Symphony Opus One embodies Dynaudio’s vision for the future of immersive audio. With a striking 73-inch wingspan, the soundbar pairs seamlessly with 83-inch TVs while integrating advanced sound technologies for music, movies, and gaming. Its design doesn’t just emphasize performance but also aesthetics, with 72 motorized wooden fins that move independently to indicate listening modes, doubling as a modern design statement.

‍

Performance That Redefines Immersive Sound

Behind its innovative design lies powerful engineering. Symphony Opus One features:

  • 24 Drive Units: Including six soft-dome tweeters, 14 mid/bass drivers, and four dual-diaphragm force-canceling subwoofers.
  • 1500 Watts of Amplification: Internal digital amplification powers the system.
  • Custom DSP Technology: Dynaudio’s proprietary spatial-audio processing expands the soundstage, creating an enveloping 3D audio effect.

The most notable upgrade in this version is the inclusion of all amplification and processing within the soundbar itself. This design streamlines setup by eliminating the need for external components, reducing clutter and simplifying the installation process.

‍

‍

Sound Modes Tailored to Every Experience

Symphony Opus One offers four distinct listening modes, each catering to a unique audio experience:

  • Authentic: A true stereo mode delivering high-fidelity sound, rooted in Dynaudio’s commitment to pure audio.
  • Soundstage: Expands stereo mixes, offering a spacious and open sound field for all listeners in the room.
  • Immersive: Delivers native Dolby Atmos and other spatial audio formats, directing sound to envelop listeners from all directions.
  • Deep Dive: Creates virtual surround sound from any source material, allowing users to enjoy immersive audio from any seating position.

These modes ensure the system adapts seamlessly to various content, whether it’s music, movies, or gaming.

‍

‍

Thoughtful Design and Usability

Dynaudio has incorporated thoughtful details to enhance both form and function:

  • Premium Materials: An aluminum-alloy frame with Nordic-inspired fabric and motorized white-oak fins creates a sleek and tactile finish.
  • Smart Optimization: A microphone embedded in the remote helps the soundbar calibrate its acoustics based on placement—whether wall-mounted, on a stand, or in open spaces.
  • Future Expansion: Plans for wireless subwoofer and rear speaker support highlight Dynaudio’s commitment to extending functionality.

Availability and Anticipation

Currently in development, the Symphony Opus One is projected to launch in 2025 with an estimated price tag of $20,000 USD, depending on customization options. For those eager to learn more, updates are available at show.dynaudio.com/symphony-opus-one.

As an all-in-one solution, Symphony Opus One demonstrates Dynaudio’s drive to simplify home audio without compromising quality. The CES 2025 showcase promises to provide an exciting glimpse into the future of immersive sound.
